parts.text: the first thing i ever shipped was a pygame loop in 2020
. now i am working to solve hard problems across any domain with software and 1st principles.
and package the work to easily be used in different product spaces
. as a software engineer, i've built in devops
, etc. they might look unrelated, but they all force me to think deeply.
i learn by building, so i have a lot of
can't debug my slop, i like to
. i am always looking to talk & build with others.

references.1: Started with Tkinter turtle, then PyGame, then HTML5 Canvas. Returned to Python with much more ambitious ideas in AI.
references.2: VLMs, MLMs, SLMs. Deep-diving in VLMs right now.
references.3: Raw video, EHR notes, voice, logs — where most of the real signal actually lives.
references.4: Mesh — 600 users over summer 2025. Centren Health — S3-backed clinical pipelines serving 8 hospital partners.
references.5: Cloud migration @ Datanexxt; infra & LLM APIs @ Undergraduation.com. Scalable systems are a must-know.
references.6: 3E8 Robotics — autonomous multi-floor delivery; elevator navigation, payload handoff. Backed by Founders Inc., based in SF. Also: deformables & generative serial chains as side work.
references.7: Clinical transformers & PK/PD modeling @ Centren Health, NYC. Osler — RAG onboarding for hospitalists, 3rd place @ RUHealthHack.
references.8: Never took a tutorial. What I've made is what I know.
references.10: Updating irregularly since summer 2021 — AI (tech & philosophy), programming & life.
references.11: Completely offline. Mainly Camelback in the Poconos.
references.12: There's something satisfying about building things with your own hands.
references.13: White rice, black beans, chicken al pastor ×2, tomatillo red chili, roasted chili corn, cheese, light sour cream.
